parts.answer: A description that includes six of the following points: • dependent variable identified is time for indicator to change colour (1) • use 5 temperatures in the range 0ºC to 60ºC /five stated temperatures in the range (1) • allow to equilibrate to (stated) temperature(s) (1) • use of same strain/species/variety/age/mass of yeast/ volume of culture (1) • add glucose/sucrose/sugar/respiratory substate (1) • same volume/mass of indicator (1) • Use a {stopwatch/timer} to measure the time for colour change (1) Ignore any stated colour change Ignore concentration Ignore nutrients Ignore amount/number of drops accept start the stopwatch and stop it when the colour has changed (6) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

TTC/methylene blue /DCPIP (1) (1) (Total for question 1 = 10 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • (the co-enzyme is) NAD (1) • Electrons are transferred to co enzyme (1) • (I norganic) phosphate added to ADP to form ATP (1) • by substrate level phosphorylation (1) Not Co-enzyme A / FAD Accept NAD + + H + + 2e - ---> NADH accept ADP +Pi ---> ATP not oxidative phosphorylation Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

Magnification in the range 0.37 to 0.5 (1) (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • creation of a grid (of the area) (1) • select sample {locations/ co-ordinates/points} using random number generator (1) Ignore select 5 sample sites at random accept any valid method of selecting at random sample e.g. numbers out of a bag (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark 2bii • a correct calculation (1) • 2.2/ 2.20 / 2.202 / 2.2017 (1) only accept 10.24/19.39/4.8/4.85/4.8475 (in table or in working) correct answer with no working gains two marks (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • comparing the means to show the difference in populations (1) • Use SD’s (and means) to show if the difference (between means) is significant {by t test or inspecting for overlap} Ignore comment on anomalies/validity/reliability/accuracy/precision Accept error bars for SD’s (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • pH (of water/soil) • water/soil moisture • soil minerals • light intensity • time of year/season Mark the first answer per line Accept PH/ph Ignore nutrients/humidity accept organic matter/humus (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • description of an appropriate method to control the selection of sample sites with similar values (1) not pH and buffer allow appropriate temperature method (1) (Total for question 2 = 10 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

• there is no correlation between the percentage oil remaining and the (length of) time (the tray was stored) (1) Accept the correlation coefficient for the percentage oil remaining and storage time will be 0 Candidates will express this in different ways (1) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark

• line graph with linear scale and axes labelled, with units (1) • data plotted correctly (1) • suitable line of best fit (1) X axis (number of) days Y axis mean percentage of oil left (%) Accept a curve or straight line similar to the examples below (ignore extrapolation) top range 1.05 to 1.15 bottom range 0.25 to 0.35 (3) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • correct calculation of d values (1) • -0.857 (1 ) e.g. 36 16 0 1 1 25 25 or total 104 (in table or working) accept -0.86/-0.9 must have - sign (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • the calculated value of r (0.857) is more than the critical value 0.786 ( 1) • therefore, reject the null hypothesis, so there is a correlation between the percentage oil left and the time (1) Ignore – sign on any calculated value r eject other critical values Accept any calculated value less than 0.786 so accept the null hypothesis therefore no correlation (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • serially dilute culture / dilute several times (1) • to reduce the number of bacteria in the samples/because too many bacteria to count individual colonies (1) • make a bacterial lawn on agar (1) • to separate individual bacteria (1) • keep for {fixed time/ temperature} (1) • to able to {see/count} colonies (1) • use aseptic technique (1) • to avoid contamination/competition/errors in counting (1) Accept a description Ignore streaking/swab/inoculate Accept spread known volume of bacteria 20- 35ºC /time 1- 4 days accept a technique described Ignore infection (6) (Total for question 3 = 14 marks) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • ethical concern needs to get ( informed) consent of volunteer (1) • safety prevent infection of mouthpiece ( 1) Accept students cannot be forced to participate Accept check students do not have health problem/are able to do the exercise Accept change the carbon dioxide absorber regularly (2) Number Answer Additional Guidance Mark • one group with and one without a fitness programme (1) • clear
